PR titles must match","testing":"Unit tests colocated in-module with #[cfg(test)] mod tests, integration tests under tests/ directory hitting mocked HTTP forge APIs via wiremock or httpmock","linting":"cargo fmt and cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings run in CI; anyhow possibly used at CLI layer only"},"patterns":["Trait-based abstraction (Forge trait) with per-provider struct implementations (GithubForge, and now GitlabForge/GiteaForge/AzureForge) to allow SecurityScanner to be forge-agnostic","Builder or config-driven client construction reading base_url/token from config.rs, allowing self-hosted Gitea/GitLab instances via custom base URLs","Pattern matching logic decoupled from forge specifics - operates on generic DiffContent/PullRequest structs so new forges only need to implement data-fetching and mapping to these generic structs","Integration tests using mock HTTP servers per forge to validate request/response parsing without live network calls"],"related_issues":[{"number":735,"title":"Add pluggable forge abstraction (prep for multi-forge support)","relevance":"introduced the Forge trait and refactored GitHub client behind it, direct architectural predecessor to #1205"},{"number":736,"title":"PR: Refactor GitHub client behind Forge trait","relevance":"the actual refactor PR implementing the trait boundary that #1205 will extend for new providers"}],"constraints":["New forge implementations must conform to existing Forge trait signature without breaking GitHub implementation or its tests","SecurityScanner and pattern matching code must remain forge-agnostic; no forge-specific branching allowed inside scanner logic","Must support self-hosted instances (custom base URLs) for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, and Azure DevOps, not just SaaS endpoints","Async runtime (tokio) and HTTP client (reqwest) already fixed as project-wide dependencies; new forge clients should reuse them rather than introduce alternatives","Authentication schemes differ per forge (PAT for GitHub/GitLab/Gitea, PAT+org for Azure DevOps) and must be normalized in config without leaking forge-specific auth details into core scanner"],"test_coverage":"Existing coverage concentrated on GitHub forge client and SecurityScanner pattern rules via unit tests plus one integration suite using mocked GitHub REST/GraphQL responses; no equivalent fixtures exist yet for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, or Azure DevOps APIs, meaning new integration test scaffolding (mock servers, fixture JSON payloads) will be required for each provider","library_findings":[{"library":"reqwest","version":"0.11/0.12 (workspace pinned)","relevant_api":"reqwest::Client with builder pattern, base_url composition, bearer_auth","notes":"Used for all forge HTTP clients; async client can be shared/cloned cheaply, supports per-provider auth headers needed for GitLab (PRIVATE-TOKEN), Gitea (token), Azure DevOps (Basic auth with PAT)"},{"library":"serde/serde_json","version":"1.x","relevant_api":"Deserialize derive macros for forge API response structs","notes":"Each new forge (GitLab, Gitea, Azure DevOps) has distinct JSON schemas for MRs/PRs/diffs requiring dedicated response structs and mapping functions into the internal generic PullRequest/DiffContent types"},{"library":"thiserror","version":"1.x","relevant_api":"derive(Error) for ForgeError enum variants","notes":"New forge-specific error variants (e.g. rate limiting differences, auth failure formats) should be added as variants rather than new error types to keep SecurityScanner error handling uniform"}],"approaches":[{"name":"Trait extension with per-provider modules","description":"Extend the existing Forge trait (from #735/#736) with three new implementations: forge/gitlab.rs, forge/gitea.rs, forge/azure_devops.rs, each mapping provider-specific REST responses into the shared PullRequest/DiffContent structs consumed by SecurityScanner; add a ForgeKind enum and factory function in config.rs to instantiate the correct client","pros":["Follows established architecture from #735/#736, minimal risk to existing GitHub path","SecurityScanner and pattern matching require zero changes","Each forge can be implemented and tested independently, allowing incremental PRs (one per forge)"],"cons":["Some code duplication across forge clients (pagination, retry, auth header handling) unless a shared HTTP helper is factored out","Azure DevOps API shape (org/project/repo hierarchy) does not map cleanly onto GitHub/GitLab's owner/repo model, requiring either trait signature changes or an adapter layer"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":9},{"name":"Generic HTTP forge client with declarative provider config","description":"Instead of separate structs per forge, build a single generic RestForgeClient parameterized by a ForgeProfile struct (base_url template, auth header name, response schema mapping via trait objects or enums) so adding a new forge is mostly configuration plus a response-mapping function","pros":["Reduces duplication of retry/pagination/auth boilerplate across forges","Easier to add future forges (e.g. Bitbucket) since only mapping functions need to be written","Centralizes cross-cutting concerns like rate-limit backoff in one place"],"cons":["Higher upfront complexity designing the generic abstraction; risk of over-engineering for only 3 providers","Azure DevOps's REST API differences (API versioning query param, org/project path segments) may not fit a simple template model cleanly, forcing escape hatches that undermine the abstraction's elegance","Harder to review/test in one PR; larger diff surface"],"complexity":"complex","files_touched":14},{"name":"Adapter-per-forge with shared trait but no new abstraction changes","description":"Minimal-diff approach: implement GitLab, Gitea, and Azure DevOps clients as thin adapters that reuse the existing GithubForge code where APIs are similar (GitLab and Gitea REST APIs are GitHub-like), only writing fully custom logic for Azure DevOps; ship as three separate smaller PRs gated behind a --forge CLI flag and config field","pros":["Fastest path to shipping incremental value (e.g. GitLab support alone could land first)","Lowest risk since GitHub implementation is untouched as the reference","Aligns with conventional commit scoping (feat(core): gitlab support, feat(core): gitea support, etc.) making review easier"],"cons":["Does not address code duplication long-term; future forges will repeat the same boilerplate","Azure DevOps adapter still requires significant custom work since it does not resemble GitHub/GitLab/Gitea APIs","Splitting into multiple PRs increases coordination overhead to close a single issue (#1205 references all three forges together)"],"complexity":"simple","files_touched":6}],"recommendation":"Approach 1 (Trait extension with per-provider modules) balances architectural consistency with delivery speed: it directly builds on the Forge trait groundwork from #735/#736, keeps SecurityScanner and pattern matching untouched (satisfying the core constraint), and allows GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, and Azure DevOps to be implemented and reviewed as separate, testable modules. The generic client approach (2) is elegant but premature optimization for three providers with meaningfully different API shapes (especially Azure DevOps); it can be revisited later if a fourth forge is requested. Approach 3 is a reasonable fallback if the team wants to ship incrementally, but since issue #1205 explicitly scopes all three forges together, approach 1 better matches the stated requirement while remaining maintainable."}
